observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
All concerned accountable officers with final demand letters to immediately liquidate cash advances, otherwise LCE shall enforce the immediate withholding of salaries for their total unliquidated cash advances; and
The Municipal Accountant to exert effort to locate the documents and pertinent information to those receivables/cash advances that cannot be traced. Conduct investigation to the causes of the long overdue cash advances for the purpose of booking up adjustments as the case may be.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant to investigate the dormant and unidentified balances of the Due to NGAs account to determine its nature, specific purpose and the source agency from which the funds were received. Thereafter, facilitate evaluation to determine whether the corresponding projects have been implemented and return the unused funds to the source agency, if warranted; and
The Municipal Treasurer to return to the Source Agency any unutilized funds which are no longer needed after the completion of the specific programs and projects pursuant to Item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and
The responsible Department Heads to furnish the Audit Team with copies of Memorandum of Agreement and supporting documents, for review and evaluation of the terms and conditions.

4.10	We recommended and the Local Chief executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant:
a.	To undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the above payable accounts and regularly prepare Schedule of Accounts Payable and Other Payables to keep track of the list of all the outstanding payable, creditors/suppliers, and how much is owed to substantiate the accuracy of the amount of claim against the Municipality;
b.	Prepare and maintain complete subsidiary ledger and schedule/aging of Accounts Payable and ensure that the schedule is reconciled with the balance reflected on the General Ledger and the financial statement; and
c.	Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the dormant payables to identify outstanding invalid claims, administrative or judicial, has been filed or that are not covered by perfected contracts and make a proper presentation for its possible reversion to the General fund.

5.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to require the personnel concerned to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials which shall be reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submitted to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. Make necessary adjustments, if warranted.

6.10		We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a.	Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; and
b.	The Municipal Accountant and designated General Services Officer maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory, keep track of the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

7.8		We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to submit the duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant to serve as the basis for setting up and record the beginning balance of RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding deferred liability in the books of accounts.

8.10	We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Fully utilize developmental PPAs funded under 20% DF. See to it that during the preparation of the Annual Budget for 20% Development Fund includes only PPAs which are feasible and procurement-and-implementation ready that could be accomplished within the specific timeline for completion; and
At the end of each year, review all unimplemented projects and verify whether they can be implemented in the succeeding year. Otherwise, cause the reversion or re-alignment of the appropriated funds including those savings from previous completed PPAs so that same may be used effectively and efficiently for more appropriate priority development projects.

9.7	We recommended and the Management agreed to exert effort in implementing the PPAs identified in its LDRRMFIP to increase its capabilities and preparedness to respond in case of disaster or emergencies.
Likewise, we recommend that unprogrammed appropriation in the Special Trust Fund be appropriated for disaster related activities and be included in the LDRRMFIP.

10.11	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2024 on the required analysis of dormant accounts and to comply with all the conditions and requirements of the said circular for the possible request for the write off and the proper disposal thereafter of the specified dormant receivables from the Commission on Audit.

11.15	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the HRMO and all Department Heads to prepare and timely update the logbooks to support Daily Time Records and formulate a standardized policy and set of procedures for engaging Job Order and Contract of Service workers to ensure that the hiring process is impartial and based on clear, consistent criteria to improve the existing system.

We recommended that, in response to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, create an Inventory Committee for the sole purpose of conducting a one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s PPE which shall be composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory.

We recommended and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) agreed to stop charging such administrative expenses to the 5% LDRRMF in pursuant to the provisions Section 4.3 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular instead it shall be sourced from the General Fund of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to communicate with Source Agency regarding its implementation and usage. Otherwise, the Municipality will return the fund to Source Agencies of the option to allocate the unutilized funds for other government projects.

Use the prescribed forms, require all drivers to prepare a Driver’s Trip Tickets with all information required properly supplied duly supported by the properly filled in gas slips; and

We recommended and  the  Local  Chief  Executive  agreed  to  direct  the Municipal Accountant to:

Reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds  to determine whether all transfers of funds are authorized  and  received by other funds and to ensure accuracy of its balances and be  able  to effect the necessary adjustment, if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

Conduct investigation to the causes of the long overdue cash advances for the purpose of booking up adjustments as the case may be;


   2. Management require the liquidation of cash advances  within  the  time frame required by law and institute  appropriate  measures/actions  to enforce liquidations of cash advances in accordance  with  the  afore-cited rules and regulations; and

   3.  The  Municipal  Accountant  exert  extra  effort  to  reconcile   the difference between the financial statement and the report of status of unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended and  the  Local  Chief  Executive  agreed  to  direct  the Municipal Accountant to  strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of  COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the required analysis of dormant accounts and to comply with all the conditions  and  requirements  of the said circular for the possible request for the write  off  of  the specified dormant receivables from the Commission on Audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the  Municipal  Treasurer agreed to conduct an analysis of the unreconciled balances between  their respective records,  make  necessary  adjustments,  if  any  and  conduct immediate reconciliations, and every quarter thereafter.
